name: @forwardimpact/outpost
description: Personal operations center — context from email, calendar, and knowledge assembled so preparation is continuous, not a morning scramble.
author: D. Olsson <hi@senzilla.io>

jobs.trigger: Walking into a meeting cold because the context was buried across email threads, a Slack channel, and last week's notes.
jobs.bigHire: keep track of people, projects, and threads without depending on memory.
jobs.littleHire: walk into every meeting already oriented without the morning scramble.
jobs.competesWith: morning email scan; re-reading calendar invites; personal notes and TODO lists; asking colleagues for context; accepting that some threads will be dropped

forces.push: Context-switching compounds; starting cold wastes everyone's time.
forces.pull: Continuous awareness without continuous effort — context assembled and maintained.
forces.habit: Manual morning prep routines and personal tracking systems that feel 'good enough.'
forces.anxiety: Delegating awareness to a system feels like losing control.

jobs.firedWhen: the briefing becomes noise; or simpler tools absorb enough of the preparation and tracking.
